headline: GM To Pay $12.75 Million To Settle California Privacy Case Over Driver Data Sales
summary: General Motors agreed to pay $12.75 million after California regulators said the company illegally sold OnStar driver data.

raw.headline: GM To Pay $12.75 Million To Settle California Privacy Case Over Driver Data Sales
raw.summary: General Motors agreed to pay $12.75 million after California regulators said the company illegally sold OnStar driver data.

headline: GM agrees to pay $12.75M in California driver privacy settlement
summary: General Motors has reached a privacy-related settlement with a group of law enforcement agencies led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta.

raw.headline: GM agrees to pay $12.75M in California driver privacy settlement
raw.summary: General Motors has reached a privacy-related settlement with a group of law enforcement agencies led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
